title: Initialization, Indeterminate Values, and Undefined Behavior · Lifetime Workshop
summary: A guided implementation pair receives a clean input trace and acceptance examples, then builds the first working defined-state initialization audit while narrating each size, lifetime, ownership, and cleanup decision. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of defined-state initialization audit while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Initialize every read-reachable scalar, member, padding-sensitive operation, and error-path state before use.
Avoid evaluating indeterminate values and understand when unsigned-char inspection is and is not permitted.
Explain how undefined behavior removes semantic constraints and invalidates debug-build intuition.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Initialization State Machine
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 2727. Produce bounded C evidence to read only fields proven initialized by state; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Initialize every read-reachable scalar, member, padding-sensitive operation, and error-path state before use.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use empty initialization and reviewed C23 facilities where supported while preserving explicit invariants and fallback tests.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient C23 Initialization Tools case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated C23 Initialization Tools condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop C23 Initialization Tools
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for C23 Initialization Tools: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 8298. Produce bounded C evidence to read only fields proven initialized by state; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use empty initialization and reviewed C23 facilities where supported while preserving explicit invariants and fallback tests.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop C23 Initialization Tools.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop C23 Initialization Tools, keep C23 Initialization Tools connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use empty initialization and reviewed C23 facilities where supported while preserving explicit invariants and fallback tests.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop C23 Initialization Tools, name the size, bounds, object, lifetime, owner, borrow, allocation, cleanup, representation, ABI, thread, and transfer boundary, C23 Initialization Tools precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop C23 Initialization Tools, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 8298. Produce bounded C evidence to read only fields proven initialized by state; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use empty initialization and reviewed C23 facilities where supported while preserving explicit invariants and fallback tests.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop C23 Initialization Tools, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: New syntax removes the need to reason about subobject initialization.
